{"key_event":"Colonial Office takeover and administrative consolidation","reasoning":"British takeover, expanded district administration, taxation, policing, courts, land controls, and indirect rule moved the colonial state from weak toward moderate capacity, though Depression pressures limited the gain. Civil society remained weak because Africans were excluded and chiefs were co-opted, but mission-educated welfare associations and the emerging urban mine workforce modestly increased autonomous organizing potential.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":3.3,"state_change":1.0,"state_power":5.0}
